verdict_summary: rev3 fixed HTTP/2 protocol error (render-generation now passes), but gamma frontend still rejects engine SSE markdown envelope on /ai/v2/generation — outline panel renders empty card #1 with no body, console throws 'Unable to complete request', warning text NEVER appears in UI even though engine emits it correctly in SSE response.

evidence.console_errors_excerpt: [PromptAndOutlineSettings] error generating outline: Unable to complete request (originating from 0ou9ekajjymyv.js / 0ntt4u~s2ausf.js / 00_3fxre00yx8.js stack: u4 / tS / se / cc / cu / n)

recommendation:
ENGINE-SSE-FORMAT-MISMATCH: rev3 successfully eliminates render-generation HTTP/2 protocol error (now returns 201 cleanly, 1.12s/2.03s — pass-through working as designed). However outline rendering still fails because gamma client cannot consume engine envelope rev2. HAR line 4561 shows engine emits correct SSE: `event: content
id: 1
data: ```md
data: --- 1
data: # ⚠️ 보안 경고
data: 
data: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.
data: ```

event: done
id: 2
data: stop
` — but client aborts with net::ERR_ABORTED (HAR line 5108) and console throws 'Unable to complete request'. Hypothesis: gamma frontend expects different SSE event structure (likely token-stream events with specific JSON payload schema like `data: {"type":"text","value":"..."}` rather than markdown-block fenced data: lines). Next direction: capture authentic baseline SSE response from gamma /ai/v2/generation (non-PII prompt, engine bypass) to extract exact event-format spec that frontend's GenerateOutlineStory parser expects, then re-author engine envelope rev3 to match. Card 1 chip rendered (rrn_decode_absent=true confirmed) — at least PII not leaked, but no warning surfaced to user.
notes: HTTP/2 protocol regression FIXED (path narrowing to /ai/v2/generation only worked as intended). Remaining problem is purely the SSE envelope schema mismatch on /ai/v2/generation. The 200 OK fast (20-235ms) responses with size 0.4kB and content-type text/event-stream confirm engine intercept is working. Console error pattern matches #748 root cause but at different layer — frontend SSE parser, not transport. RRN decode never appeared in any rendered card — confirmed safe. Card count chip auto-decreased from 10 to 8 between regenerate attempts (gamma client behavior, unrelated). HAR 91KB→206KB after rev3 retest contains both fix-good (render-generation 201) and fix-incomplete (generation aborted client-side) traffic.
